pr.title: Add tools/table-typer and infer more types
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Added a new Go utility, tools/table-typer, to infer concrete function signatures from table declarations and asm data, currently hard-coded for ItemStateTable. The PR also applied the tool's output across many item files, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or void(void) placeholders with bool/void Item_GObj* callback signatures. This improved header/source type precision for item state-table callbacks without changing GALE01 output.
what_changed: Introduced tools/table-typer, a Go command invoked as `go run . ItemStateTable ../../src/ ../../asm/`.
Documented that the tool finds extern declarations of a table type, locates matching data in asm files, parses table entries, and rewrites .c/.h signatures to known types.
Implemented ItemStateTable support as a 4-entry table pattern whose callback fields infer `bool (*)(Item_GObj*)`, `void (*)(Item_GObj*)`, and `bool (*)(Item_GObj*)` after an ignored enum-like field.
Implemented C signature parsing and rewriting that preserves existing parameter names when possible and supplies `gobj` when an unnamed parameter must be added.
Added type-equivalence normalization so `Item_GObj` and `Fighter_GObj` are treated as equivalent to `HSD_GObj` for comparison purposes.
Updated many item headers from `UNK_RET fn(UNK_PARAMS)` to concrete `bool fn(Item_GObj* gobj)` or `void fn(Item_GObj* gobj)` declarations.
Updated matching C definitions and static prototypes that previously used `void` parameters to accept `Item_GObj* gobj`.
Changed several `int` callback returns in `itfoods.c` to `bool`, while preserving the existing `return 0;` bodies.
Added missing item-local header includes where the signature rewrite needed declared types, including `itclinkmilk.h` and `itnesspkthundertrail.h`.
Added `[*.go] indent_style = tab` to `.editorconfig` and added an Unlicense-style license file for the new tool subdirectory.
smart_moves: Turned a one-off script from PR #1776 into a checked-in, documented tool so the table-derived signature inference can be reused and extended.
Used existing asm table data and extern `ItemStateTable` declarations as evidence for callback types instead of manually guessing each placeholder signature.
Kept the tool narrowly scoped to a hard-coded `ItemStateTable` first, but structured it with a `tableTypes` map so other table layouts could be added later.
Handled common decomp typedef noise by normalizing `Item_GObj`, `Fighter_GObj`, and `HSD_GObj` when comparing signatures.
Applied inferred signatures consistently to both headers and implementation stubs, reducing mismatches between declarations and definitions.
Preserved no-binary-change behavior; the decomp.dev report said GALE01 had no changes.
decomp_lessons: State-table layouts can be mined to recover callback signatures in bulk: for ItemStateTable, callback slots commonly correspond to `bool Item_GObj*`, `void Item_GObj*`, and `bool Item_GObj*` handlers.
Replacing `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` with concrete callback prototypes in headers can expose more type information without requiring full function decompilation.
When a tool rewrites signatures, check for needed includes afterward; the README explicitly warns that rewritten files may reference a type they do not currently include.
For item callback stubs that return false/0, `bool` is often the better table-derived return type than `int` when the function is a state-table predicate/update callback.
Tooling that parses source should tolerate decomp conventions such as address comments, placeholder prototypes, unnamed parameters, and typedef aliases.
Even no-op empty callback definitions should carry their table-derived `Item_GObj* gobj` parameter if the state table passes one.
naming_conventions: Inferred item callback parameters were generally named `gobj`, producing signatures such as `bool it_802D59D0(Item_GObj* gobj)`.
Header declarations retained address comments in the existing style, e.g. `/* 2D246C */ bool it_802D246C(Item_GObj* gobj);`.
The tool's table registry is named `tableTypes`, with entries keyed by C type name such as `ItemStateTable`.
The tool models callback signatures with `FuncType` and rewritten declarations with `FuncSignature`.
Existing symbol names were not renamed; the PR only improved return and parameter types for symbols like `it_802C8F20`, `it_802D5048`, and `it_802E3588`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The tool scans asm files for non-.text and non-.sdata sections, extracts `.global` blocks containing `.byte` data, and records `.byte`, `.2byte`, `.4byte`, and `.8byte` entries.
It maps extern table declarations from headers to asm table labels, then parses entries only when the asm table length is a multiple of the known table entry count.
For `ItemStateTable`, it skips `NULL` callback entries and only yields non-null function names with known function types.
Signature rewriting is guarded by a function-name containment check before parsing and editing each file, limiting unnecessary work.
The tool compares normalized types before rewriting so equivalent object pointer typedefs do not cause redundant or harmful edits.
The decomp.dev bot reported `No changes` for GALE01, confirming these type/signature edits did not alter the built binary.

review_feedback.feedback: Asked the author to add a copyleft or permissive license to the new tool subdirectory.
review_feedback.resolution: The PR added `tools/table-typer/LICENSE`, and the author replied `done`; a later review approved the PR.

review_feedback.feedback: Noted that the linter was unhappy because the new tool was written in Go rather than Python.
review_feedback.resolution: The PR added `.editorconfig` settings for Go files and proceeded to approval.

follow_up_queries: Find future PRs that extend tools/table-typer beyond ItemStateTable.
Search for remaining `extern ItemStateTable` declarations whose callback functions still use `UNK_RET` or `UNK_PARAMS`.
Search item source files for table-derived callbacks still declared as `void fn(void)` or `bool fn(void)`.
Check whether table-typer include warnings caused later follow-up fixes for missing `Item_GObj` includes.
Compare PR #1776 with PR #1784 to understand the original script workflow that was rewritten.
